threats.scenario: RSA private key is hardcoded in lib/insecurity.ts:22. Any attacker with read access to the source code (public repo or source disclosure) can forge arbitrary JWT tokens, impersonate any user including admin, with 6-hour validity.
threats.controls_in_place: JWT signed RS256 — but private key exposed in source

remediation.title: Remove hardcoded RSA private key; inject via environment/vault
steps: Remove private key literal from insecurity.ts
Load private key from environment variable or mounted secret

threats.scenario: Login endpoint POST /rest/user/login uses raw SQL string interpolation in routes/login.ts:35: SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password)}'. Attacker can bypass authentication entirely using SQL injection payload like email=' OR 1=1--

remediation.title: Replace raw SQL in login with Sequelize findOne with where clause
steps: Replace models.sequelize.query with UserModel.findOne({ where: { email, password: security.hash(password) }})
Remove all raw SQL string interpolation

threats.scenario: Passwords hashed with MD5 (lib/insecurity.ts:43). MD5 is cryptographically broken for password storage — rainbow tables and GPU cracking trivially recover passwords. Any database dump or SQL injection exposes all user passwords.

remediation.title: Replace MD5 password hashing with bcrypt or Argon2

threats.scenario: No rate limiting on POST /rest/user/login (server.ts:594 — only /rest/user/reset-password has rate limiting). Attacker can brute-force credentials at unrestricted speed. Combined with MD5 hashing, even weak passwords are quickly discovered.

remediation.title: Add rate limiting to POST /rest/user/login
steps: Add rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 10 }) before the login() handler
Consider account lockout after N consecutive failures

threats.scenario: HMAC key hardcoded in lib/insecurity.ts:44 ('pa4qacea4VK9t9nGv7yZtwmj'). Used for coupon validation and deluxe token computation. Attacker knowing this key can forge valid deluxe membership tokens and coupons.
threats.controls_in_place: HMAC applied but with publicly known key

remediation.title: Remove hardcoded HMAC secret; load from environment variable
steps: Move HMAC secret to environment variable HMAC_SECRET
Load via process.env.HMAC_SECRET with validation at startup

threats.scenario: Cookie parser uses hardcoded weak secret 'kekse' (server.ts:289). While JWT is the primary auth mechanism, the cookie secret could enable session token manipulation. express-jwt version 0.1.3 (package.json) is critically outdated and vulnerable to algorithm confusion attacks (alg:none bypass).
threats.controls_in_place: RS256 JWT signing (partially mitigates alg:none if enforced)

remediation.title: Update express-jwt and jsonwebtoken to current versions; secure cookie secret
steps: Enforce algorithm RS256 explicitly in token verification
Set strong random cookie secret from environment variable

threats.scenario: Winston logger (lib/logger.ts) uses simple text format without structured fields (no request-id, user-id, action, outcome). Security events (login success/failure, password reset) are not explicitly logged as security events, making audit trail reconstruction difficult.
threats.controls_in_place: Morgan access logging + Winston console logging

remediation.title: Add structured security event logging with user context
steps: Log login attempts (success/failure), password changes, and privilege escalation with user-id, IP, timestamp
